articles.summary: Morocco will see seasonally hot weather on Sunday, with temperatures approaching 40°C in the southeast, eastern regions and parts of the Sahara, the General Directorate of Meteorology said. articles.title: Oman condemns attack on its territory
articles.summary: Oman condemns attack on its territory The Omani government has strongly condemned an attack on its territory, state media said. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it targeted Oman during a barrage aimed at countries across the Gulf. The Oman News Agency said on X that "the Sultanate of Oman affirms its condemnation and denunciation of this attack", which came just hours after the country hosted Iran's foreign minister to address security issues in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Kuwait military says responding to aerial attacks
articles.summary: Kuwait military says responding to aerial attacks Kuwait's military said its air defence forces were working to intercept an ongoing attack on its territory, as countries across the Gulf were targeted by Iranian drones and missiles.  "The General Staff of the Army notes that any explosion sounds heard are the result of air defence systems intercepting hostile attacks," the military said in a statement posted on social media.

Jordan army says three Iranian missiles fell inside country
articles.summary: Jordan army says three Iranian missiles fell inside country Jordan's army has said that three Iranian missiles fell on the kingdom's territory at dawn today.  "Three missiles fired from Iranian territory fell at dawn in several locations across the kingdom, without causing any casualties. The damage is limited to minor material losses," said a military source quoted in an army statement.

articles.title: One Indian national missing after attack on vessel off Oman
articles.summary: One Indian national missing after attack on vessel off Oman One Indian national is missing after an attack on the commercial vessel GFS Galaxy off the coast of Oman earlier on Sunday, the Ministry of External Affairs said. "Of the 11 Indian nationals on board, 10 have been rescued so far, while one Indian national is reportedly missing," the ministry said, while condemning the attack. articles.summary: Morning recap Good morning, Middle East Eye readers. The United States launched a third round of strikes on Iran early Sunday, saying it had hit a total of 149 military targets across the country. Iran responded by claiming attacks on US military infrastructure across several Gulf states. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) also declared the Strait of Hormuz closed "until further notice" and warned that Tehran would not tolerate foreign interference in the strategic waterway. articles.title: Lindsey Graham, key Trump ally, dies after sudden illness
articles.summary: Lindsey Graham, key Trump ally, dies after sudden illness US Republican senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of Donald Trump, a staunch Israel supporter, and a foreign policy hawk who advocated for military intervention against Iran, has died from a sudden illness, his office said in the early hours of Sunday. He had just turned 71. He had served in the Senate since 2003, representing South Carolina. In May this year,  Graham threatened Saudi Arabia and other Arab and Muslim countries with joining the Abraham Accords as a price to pay for ending the Israeli-US war on Iran.

articles.summary: Hormuz escalation: Five Iranian cities hit, Qatar says three injured US forces struck five cities in Iran’s Bushehr province, including Asaluyeh, Dir, Bushehr city, Dashti and Tangestan, IRNA reported. Mohammad Mozaffari, the governor of Bushehr, told IRNA: "Following the ceasefire violation by the American-Zionist enemy, four points in this city were hit by enemy projectiles." “A total of 10 enemy projectiles hit three areas in Bushehr city and one area around the city of Choghadak, which is part of Bushehr city," he said and added the attacks were recorded between 2.45 am and 3 am local time

articles.title: UKMTO says crew of container ship struck off Omani coast rescued
articles.summary: UKMTO says crew of container ship struck off Omani coast rescued The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said the crew of a container ship attacked off the coast of Oman earlier had been "rescued by local authorities". An Iranian missile strike on the vessel, identified by US Central Command (Centcom) as the Cyprus-flagged M/V GFS Galaxy, was cited by Washington as the trigger for a third round of US military strikes on Iran. The UKMTO had earlier reported that a fire had broken out on board the vessel and that the crew had abandoned ship in a lifeboat.

articles.summary: Israel uses land takeover orders to expand settler control, commission says Since the start of 2026, Israeli authorities have issued 49 military orders to seize 2,093 dunums (517 acres) of Palestinian land under the guise of security measures, WAFA reported. Citing a position paper released on Saturday by the Wall and Settlement Resistance Commission, the report said the orders do not formally transfer ownership from Palestinian landowners. Ghalibaf's post was accompanied by an image of Article 5 of the memorandum of understanding (MoU), which concerns the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. articles.summary: IRGC hits Jordan's Prince Hassan Air Base, Qatar 'intercepts missile' The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said its aerospace force had targeted US military facilities at Jordan's Prince Hassan Air Base, according to Iran's state broadcaster, IRIB. The IRGC said it fired several missiles at the base, claiming to have destroyed a command-and-control centre and hangars housing MQ-9 drones. In an update, Qatar's Ministry of Defence said its armed forces had "intercepted a missile attack which targeted" the country. articles.summary: UAE engaging with Iranian drones and missiles, defence ministry says The UAE's air defences are currently engaging with missile attacks and incoming drones from Iran, the country's defence ministry said in a statement on X. The announcement came after the US struck dozens of targets across several Iranian cities following a missile strike by the IRGC Navy on a Cyprus-flagged ship in the Strait of Hormuz. articles.title: Crew abandon Cyprus-flagged ship hit in Hormuz
articles.summary: Crew abandon Cyprus-flagged ship hit in Hormuz The crew of a Cyprus-flagged container ship that was damaged in a missile strike by the IRGC Navy in the Strait of Hormuz has abandoned the vessel and boarded a lifeboat, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said. The UKMTO said the update came from military authorities and the vessel's company security officer. The ship, identified by US Central Command (Centcom) as the M/V GFS Galaxy, was transiting the Strait of Hormuz when it was struck.  Vessels are seen anchored in Bandar Abbas along the Strait of Hormuz in a picture obt

articles.summary: Four settlers arrested for attacking CNN crew in West Bank Israeli police said they arrested four suspects on Saturday for damaging a vehicle carrying foreign journalists in the occupied West Bank. US broadcaster CNN had described it as an attack on its crew by Israeli settlers. According to CNN, its team was reporting near the village of Sinjil, north of Ramallah, when the incident took place. articles.title: UKMTO advises ships to transit with caution, report 'suspicious activity'
articles.summary: UKMTO advises ships to transit with caution, report 'suspicious activity' The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) advised vessels to transit with caution and report any suspicious activity after an Iranian missile struck a Cyprus-flagged ship in the Strait of Hormuz. The attack on the vessel was followed by a fresh round of US strikes on several Iranian cities. The UKMTO said it had received a report of "an incident 9NM east of Oman".
